prompt:
Migrating a decade of browser bookmarks out of three different browsers and I want ONE canonical vault with a CLI over it. Python 3, stdlib only, `--db PATH` JSON file on every command. Modules I insist on because other scripts of mine will import them: `urlnorm.py` (URL canonicalization, pure), `netscape.py` (the bookmark HTML format, pure parse/render), `cli.py` as the entry point, plus a store module of your choosing. URL canonicalization -- `urlnorm.normalize(url) -> str`: - lowercase scheme and host; strip default ports (:80 http, :443 https) - drop the #fragment entirely - strip one trailing slash from the path ('/x/' -> '/x'), and a bare '/' path collapses to no path at all - sort query parameters by key, STABLE (equal keys keep their relative order), '?a=1&b=2' style - no scheme at all means https CLI, dedupe is by normalized URL: - `add URL [--title T] [--tag TAG]...` -- new URL: requires --title, prints `added `. URL already in the vault (post-normalization!): prints `merged `, unions the tags in, replaces the title only if --title was given. Tags are normalized on the way in: lowercase, trimmed, internal whitespace runs become a single '-', empties dropped. - `tag URL TAG...` -- add tags to an existing bookmark (same normalization); unknown URL: nonzero exit, stderr. - `search [TERM] [--tag TAG]...` -- case-insensitive substring match of TERM against title OR normalized url (no TERM = match everything); every --tag must be present (AND). Output one per line sorted by url: `URLTITLEtag1,tag2` with tags sorted; empty third field if untagged. No matches: nothing, exit 0. - `export --json PATH` / `import --json PATH` -- a JSON list of {url, title, tags}, export sorted by url with sorted tags. JSON import is for LEGACY dumps: normalize+dedupe the urls (later entries win the title, tags union) but keep the tags VERBATIM, garbage and all -- that's what repair is for. - `export --html PATH` / `import --html PATH` -- Netscape bookmark format via netscape.py. HTML import merges like add (tags normalized). - `repair` -- normalize every tag already in the vault (same rules as add), dedupe within each bookmark, then print `repaired N bookmarks` where N counts bookmarks whose tag set actually changed. `netscape.render(bookmarks)` takes [{url,title,tags}] and emits exactly: Bookmarks

Bookmarks

title

one DT line per bookmark (4-space indent), sorted by url, tags comma-joined sorted, TAGS attribute omitted entirely for untagged bookmarks, proper HTML escaping in both attributes and the title text, trailing newline at the end. `netscape.parse(text)` inverts it and must also survive real-browser junk:

folder lines get ignored (we flatten), whitespace varies, entities need unescaping. Everything must survive a process restart via the db file. `python3 test_bookmarks.py` is the contract; hands off that file.
